name: BLACK Phantom Turbo Complete
source_of_truth: ptcg_engine source + CABT official wrapper contracts
count_decisions: Drakloak is raised to four because Recon Directive is the repeatable hand-quality engine, each copy activates independently, and it is the stable non-Candy path to the next attacker.
Dragapult ex is set to three because Dawn and Tera Orb already provide direct Stage 2 access; the fourth Drakloak improves more turns than the fourth terminal evolution card.
Duskull remains three because the bomb line is a converter, not the main attacker, and four copies overfill the five-slot Bench while competing for Rare Candy.
Dusclops and Dusknoir remain 2-2 because 50 and 130 are distinct Prize-routing tools; Dusclops must not always be skipped.
Energy is Fire 6 / Psychic 7. Thirteen total remains dense with four Cinderace, two Crispin and repeatable Drakloak filtering; the freed slot becomes a third Dawn.
Night Stretcher is raised to two because both Cursed Blast stages self-KO and the same card can recover a missing Basic Energy colour.
opening_plan: Prefer Cinderace as the setup Active when offered.
Buddy-Buddy Poffin takes Dreepy plus Duskull first, then a second Dreepy; Azelf enters only after the damage reservoir matters.
Turbo Flare selects only Energy that completes real Fire-Psychic Dragapult routes and may select fewer than three rather than overattach.
Do not spend Rare Candy on Dusknoir while the first Dragapult attack route is incomplete unless Cursed Blast immediately converts a Prize.
midgame_plan: Use every legal Drakloak before irreversible hand commitments when Recon Directive can improve the route; select one of the top two and bottom the lower-value card.
Complete the first Dragapult with one Fire and one Psychic, then reserve all surplus Energy for the second attacker or a proven Azelf finish.
Phantom Dive allocates six counters one at a time toward immediate KO, high-Prize targets, 130 and 50 Cursed Blast bands, then the lowest remaining HP.
Dusclops and Dusknoir are not activated merely because legal. Their self-KO must take a Prize, enable the active Dragapult KO, or create an energy-ready Azelf terminal.
Rare Candy priority is attack-ready Dragapult, positive-Prize Dusknoir, backup Dragapult, then Cinderace recovery.
endgame_plan: Prime Catcher is the preferred Cinderace-to-Dragapult handoff plus gust action.
Azelf converts the opposing damage reservoir into a one-Prize lethal route only when Psychic plus Colorless is already funded.
Boss's Orders is reserved for confirmed Prize conversion.
Do not attach duplicate colours to a ready Dragapult unless Search proves immediate retreat or attack value.

effect_contracts.Drakloak: effect.id=120 resolves current.looking area 12, keeps the highest live-route card and bottoms the other.
effect_contracts.Phantom Dive: effect.id=121 and context=14 choose an opposing Bench target six times; each ten-damage-counter choice is evaluated against KO and 50/130/200 breakpoints.
effect_contracts.Dusclops: effect.id=132 and context=13 select one opposing Pokemon for five counters after self-KO; activation fails closed without an immediate conversion.
effect_contracts.Dusknoir: effect.id=133 and context=13 select one opposing Pokemon for thirteen counters after self-KO; activation fails closed without an immediate conversion.
effect_contracts.Crispin: effect.id=1198 selects different Basic Energy and attaches the remaining Energy to the exact individual line missing that colour.
effect_contracts.Rare Candy: effect.id=1079 and context=37 resolve Stage 2 plus exact Basic target; attack-ready Dragapult outranks nonterminal Dusknoir development.
effect_contracts.Turbo Flare: effect.id=666 selects only the required Fire/Psychic cards and attaches each to an incomplete Bench route.

search_package.reason: Role-specific search supports two Stage 2 lines while four Drakloak provide repeatable hand quality without discard-heavy generic search.

required_transition_captures: Drakloak ACTIVATE, LOOKING to hand, and deck-bottom selection
Turbo Flare selective energy and exact Bench targets
